title: fix: dedupe activity dashboard events
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
## Summary
- Dedupes Activity usage and earnings endpoint reads before charts, transactions, and CSV rows are returned.
- Removes unused `d1_rewards.datasource` and `quest_usage_summary.pipe` from observability definitions.
- Keeps the live duplicate materialized rows in place; endpoints now collapse duplicate logical events at read time.

## Validation
- Staging/prod Tinybird dry-runs for the dedupe endpoint changes passed before deployment.
- Deployed endpoint-only dedupe change to staging deployment `51` and prod deployment `139`.
- Verified prod duplicate source rows: materialized `activity_usage_events` has 2 rows for the known event, live endpoint returns 1.
- Rechecked repo consumers: no references to `quest_usage_summary` or `d1_rewards` remain.
- Destructive cleanup dry-runs showed only `d1_rewards` datasource deletion, `quest_usage_summary` pipe deletion, and read-token permission cleanup.
- Deployed destructive cleanup to staging deployment `52` and prod deployment `140` with `--allow-destructive-operations`.
- Verified `quest_usage_summary` now returns 404 and `d1_rewards` is not found in both staging and prod.
- Smoke-tested the four Activity endpoints after prod cleanup; all return 200.

## Notes
- The known duplicate materialized usage rows were not physically deleted. A simple Tinybird delete condition matches both identical rows, not only the extra copy, so the safer cleanup is read-time dedupe.
- `d1_rewards` was empty in both staging and prod before deletion.
- `quest_usage_summary` had no repo consumer; prod returned rows only because the pipe itself existed.

gist.headline: Double vision cured
gist.blurb: We've polished up the Activity dashboard to filter out duplicate usage and earnings events. Your charts and transaction histories are now perfectly crisp and accurate.
gist.summary: Activity usage and earnings endpoints now deduplicate events at read time, ensuring charts, transactions, and CSV exports display accurately.
gist.impact: Users will see precise, deduplicated data in their Activity dashboard instead of double-counted events.
gist.visual_concept: The bee mascot wearing glasses or holding a magnifying glass, looking at a clean, organized chart or ledger. A robot companion sweeping away duplicate pixelated data blocks.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing tiny reading glasses, inspecting a clean, glowing lime green (#ecf874) bar chart on a retro monitor. A chunky robot companion sweeps away duplicate pixelated data blocks with a small broom. Soft pastel gradient background, warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm and organized.
